title: Modi-Sheikh Mohamed Meet at G7: India-UAE Partnership Enters Third Summit in 2026
lede: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan at the G7 Summit in Evian, France on 16 June 2026—their third meeting this year—to review bilateral cooperation in technology, trade, investment, energy, and defence while emphasizing regional stability in West Asia.

sections.context: The meeting between Modi and Sheikh Mohamed represents an intensification of high-level diplomatic engagement between India and the UAE in 2026. This was the third bilateral meeting between the two leaders within the calendar year, following Sheikh Mohamed's January visit to India and Modi's May visit to the UAE. The G7 Summit platform provided an opportunity for the two leaders to review progress flowing from these recent state visits and exchange views on regional and global developments affecting both nations. The meeting underscores the centrality of the India-UAE Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in India's West Asia diplomacy, with bilateral cooperation advancing across multiple sectors including technology, trade, investment, energy, and defence.
sections.threads: The Modi-Sheikh Mohamed meeting reflects broader momentum in the India-UAE strategic relationship that has crystallized around energy cooperation and investment frameworks. [[india-ae-pms-eighth-uae-visit-in-12-years-deepens-energy-coopera-2026|Modi's May 2026 visit to the UAE deepened energy cooperation]], establishing mechanisms for strategic crude oil storage and energy security arrangements. Beyond bilateral ties, Modi's emphasis on dialogue, diplomacy, and respect for international law signals India's positioning on regional stability in West Asia—a theme increasingly central to India-UAE coordination. The Prime Minister's call for continued free and safe navigation through the Strait of Hormuz reflects shared economic interests; both nations depend critically on unimpeded maritime commerce through this chokepoint. [[india-ae-india-condemns-fujairah-attack-calls-for-hormuz-shippin-2026|India has previously reaffirmed commitment to freedom of navigation in the Hormuz corridor]], making this a consistent bilateral talking point.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor whether the India-UAE partnership produces formal agreements or joint statements on West Asia stability in the coming months, particularly regarding Hormuz transit and regional maritime security. The frequency of Modi-Sheikh Mohamed meetings in 2026—three times in six months—suggests Delhi is elevating UAE engagement as a lynchpin of its West Asia strategy; watch for whether this translates into expanded defence cooperation or joint diplomatic initiatives on regional disputes. Energy frameworks established during Modi's May visit merit close monitoring: implementation timelines for crude oil storage arrangements and LPG supply agreements will indicate whether partnership architecture moves from announcement to operational reality. [[india-ae-uae-presidents-high-level-visit-to-india-signals-strate-2026|Sheikh Mohamed's January 2026 visit signaled strategic intent]]; the subsequent Modi visit and now the G7 meeting suggest a deliberate diplomatic rhythm designed to institutionalize cooperation rather than rely on episodic summits.
